hints.Christus: 'Christus' is a noun meaning 'the Christ / the Anointed One'

hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' — 'es' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go out: That would be 'exire' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to follow: That would be 'sequi' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive of 'esse' would be 'sis' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'eras' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'eris' — 'es' is present indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) which also has infinitive 'esse'
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'te' — 'Tu' is nominative, the subject
hints.vocative: While 'tu' can be vocative, here it is emphatic nominative subject of 'es'

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'Christum' — 'Christus' is predicate nominative with 'es'
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'Christe' — 'Christus' is predicate nominative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Christi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Christi' ends in -i
hints.indeclinable: 'Christus' clearly declines: Christus, Christi, Christo, Christum, Christo

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Dei' is genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' is genitive, showing relation

translation: You are the Christ of God
